Mampujan.Synopsis Text:
Year 2000, a town in Montes de María, Colombia, is accused of collaborating with a guerrilla group, and suffers an incursion by paramilitaries, in which they carry out massacres, and produce a massive displacement of hundreds of families.

In the midst of it all, a group of women, the Mampuján Weavers, make tapestries as trauma therapy, memory reconstruction and commitment to peace.
Mampujan.Description:
Original tapestries. Weavers of Mampuján 2009

Embroidered in fabric,

Courtesy of the Meeting Points Foundation

Externado de Colombia University

Memory.description:
When it comes to memory, what hurts is that which finds no place in the weave. What can’t be mended and remains as a frayed hole.

Again and again we settle a hand on the painful spot, as dogs lick their wounds.

Sometimes we can darn it. And the tongue that licked now begins to speak.
